reasoning: Superman benefits from a prime July 11 release date, which ensures its complete domestic run will count toward the 2025 calendar gross. This provides a structural advantage against major late-year competitors like Zootopia 2 (Nov 26) and Avatar 3 (Dec 19), whose massive totals will be split into 2026. However, history shows a late release can still dominate (Avatar 2 earned ~$400M in its 2022 calendar window alone). To secure the #1 spot, Superman must outgross Avatar 3's initial sprint, Zootopia 2's 5-week sprint, and mid-year juggernauts like Jurassic World Rebirth and Captain America: Brave New World. Given the recent historical sluggishness of the DC brand, a runaway hit ($500M+ domestic) is an uphill climb, although James Gunn's reboot raises the ceiling. The PM baseline of 7% accurately prices the odds of this specific, high-upside breakout scenario.
